items.name: Hypotension
information: ● Defined as SBP < 70 + 2 x (age in years)

items.name: Endotracheal Tube Depth
information: ● appropriate endotracheal tube depth should be confirmed by Chest X-Ray as soon as feasible.

information: ** King Airway sizes 0, 1, 2, and 2.5 are not FDA approved for use in the US

items.vals: 3,Premature / Small Neonate|40 mm|Pink|Guedel Size 000,4,Neonate|50 mm|Blue|Guedel Size 00,11,Infant|60 mm|Black|Guedel Size 0,18,Toddler|70 mm|White|Guedel Size 1,22,Child|80 mm|Green|Guedel Size 2,43,Older Child / Small Adult|90 mm|Yellow|Guedel Size 3

items.name: Premedications
notes: ● Defasculation unnecessary <5 yo.
● Atropine - some experts recommend <5 yo, other recommend only if <= 1 yo.

items.name: Vecuronium IV/IO Bolus
information: ● 1 mg/mL when reconstituted with 10 mL of sterile water.
● Duration can be unpredictable and prolonged at this dose.

items.name: Induction Medications
notes: ● Consider decreasing the induction dose in hypotensive patients

items.name: Etomidate IV/IO
information: Risk of Adrenal Suppression, Max dose 20 mg

items.name: Ketamine IM
information: Can use with Succinylcholine IM if no IV access

items.name: Midazolam IV/IO
information: Risk of decreased BP, HR, RR

items.name: Propofol IV/IO
information: Risk of decreased BP, HR, RR

items.name: Paralytics
notes: ● Considering increasing paralytics doses in hypotensive patients.
● Succinylcholine and Rocuronium are preferred for RSI due to rate of onset, shorter duration.

items.name: Succinylcholine IV/IO
information: ● Avoid in renal failure, suspected hyperkalemia

items.name: Succinylcholine IM
information: ● Use with Ketamine IM if no IV access
Avoid in renal failure, suspected hyperkalemia

items.name: Vecuronium IV/IO
information: ● 1 mg/mL when reconstituted with 10 mL of sterile water.
● Duration can be unpredictable and prolonged at this dose.

items.name: Atracurium IV/IO
information: use lower end of dosing if age < 2 years

items.name: Sugammadex IV/IO
information: ● for emergent rocuronium and vecuronium reversal
● caution in patients with cardiac disease

items.name: Fentanyl Infusion
information: ● Concentrations vary based on each pharmacy, confirm concentration before initiating any infusions.

items.name: Hydromorphone Infusion
information: ● Concentrations vary for each pharmacy, confirm concentration before initiating any infusions.

items.name: Dexmedetomidine  IV/IO Bolus
information: ● 200 mcg / 2 mL requires dilution before administration

items.name: Tidal Volume
information: ● adjust to plateau pressure < 35 cm H2O

items.name: Mode
information: ● use cuffed ETT for volume-targeted ventilation

items.name: FiO2
information: ● initiate at 100% and ween down as tolerated

items.name: I:E Ratio
information: ● Obstructive 1:4 (permissive hypercapnia to avoid breath stacking)

items.name: Avoid Peak Pressures > 40
information: ● Adjust to plateau pressure < 35 cm H2O

steps.algostep:
● Verbal re-direction
● Distraction 
● Age-appropropriate direction
●  Set reasonable limits and explain consequences

● 1-on-1 verbal support
● Open-ended questions
● Listen without rushing
● Remain calm
● Keep at leat a 2-arm length distance
● Keep a calm demeanor, facial expression and posture
● Ask about specific techniques or tools that have worked in the past

● Reward cooperation
● Active listening
● Build empathy

items.name: Guidelines
notes: ● Based on presumed etiology for agitation

steps.algostep:
● Extra home medication dose or
● Olanzapine or
● Diphenhydramine

items.name: Restraint Use
notes: ● Only use when necessary for patient / staff safety and less restrictive options have failed.
● Remove restraints as soon as safe to do so.

items.name: Midazolam Intranasal
information: Use higher concentration for IN dosing when available

items.name: Lorazepam PO/IM/IV
information: **For use in refractory agitation or in setting of drug shortages**-

items.name: Anaphylaxis / Allergic Reaction
notes: ● Note: IV epinephrine bolus dosing is no longer recommended to treat anaphylaxis

items.name: Epinephrine Nebulized
information: ● 2.5-5 mg Epi (1 mg/mL,1:1,000) mixed with 4-5 mL Normal Saline OR
● Weight-Based: 0.5 mg/kg Epi (1 mg/mL, 1:1000) (Only for use in young infants, max 5 mg)

items.name: Metoclopromide PO
information: ● comes as 1 mg/mL solution, or 5 and 10 mg tabs, max dose 10 mg

items.name: Ondansetron ODT PO
information: oral dissolving tablet, comes as 4 mg and 8 mg tabs, max dose 8 mg

items.name: Promethazine  IM/IV
information: ●  IV/IM is generally avoided due to high risk of tissue injury.
●  If used, promethazine should be administered diluted and infused over 10-15 minutes or at a rate not to exceed 25 mg/min.
● Adminster through a large bore vein (not hand or wrist) or via a running IV line at port farthest from patient's vein to reduce risk of phlebitis.

items.name: Promethazine PO/PR
information: This is also the rectal dose. PO also available in 12.5,25, and 50 mg tabs. Max dose 25 mg

items.name: Dexamethasone
information: ● Dosing for Peri-Operative nausea and vomiting

items.name: Antimicrobials (IV/IM)
notes: ● Disclaimer: All antimicrobial doses are based on manufacture recommended dosing for their most common indicated uses.
● Confirmation of doses is recommended prior to medication administration.
● Adjustment of dose may be required in the event of renal insufficiency or failure, or with hepatic disease.

items.name: Acyclovir  IV/IO
information: ● For most common infections including Varicella, herpes zoster, HSV encephalitis
● higher doses may be indicated in certain conditions.

items.name: Ampicillin IV/IO/IM
information: ● Dosing is complex for neonates and depends on gestational and postnatal age.
● Max dose can upwards of 8-12 grams/day.

dosages.label: ● 150 mg/kg/day divided Q8 hr; 200 mg/kg/day divided Q6 hr

items.name: Gentamicin IV/IO/IM
information: ● Option 1: (Initial dose) adhere to policy guidelines, specific indications, and level to guide further dosing. For CNS infection, intra-abdominal infection, UTI, endocarditis
● Option 2: Represents extended-interval dosing. Please refer to your institutional guidelines.

items.name: Metronidazole IV
information: 30 mg/kg/day IV Q6 hr (7.5 mg/kg/dose), max dose 4 grams/day

dosages.label: ● Severe Pseudomonas Infection: 75-100 mg/kg Q6 hr

items.name: Vancomycin IV/IO
information: ● Initial dosing presented, adhere to policy and institutional guidelines, specific indications, and levels to guide further dosing.

items.name: Antimicrobials (Oral)
notes: Calculated dosages are made with assumed normal renal and hepatic function.  Contact your Pharmacist for necessary dose adjustments.

dosages.label: ● Urinary tract infections, skin/soft tissue infections:
● Systemic infections, intraabdominal infections, invasive Pseudomonas infections:

items.name: Doxycycline PO
information: Doxycycline binds to calcium less readily than other tetracyclines, has not been shown to cause teeth staining

items.name: Zanamvir Inhaled
information: not recommended < 7 years old  Prophylaxis duration 7 days, not recommended < 5 years old

items.name: Zanamivir Inhaled (Prophylaxis))
information: Influenza prophylaxis, duration 7 days, not recommended < 5 years old

steps.algostep: * Rate (if patient < 20 kg, add maintenance fluid)
* Rate (if patient < 20 kg, add maintenance fluid)
● Parkland formula plus maintenance fluids, used in patients who weigh < 20 kg
●  3-4 mL lactated ringers * weight * %TBSA burned (second and third degree)
● Half administered over the first 8 hours (time of injury), remaining half administered over the next 16 hours.
● plus 1 mL/kg/hr for each kg more than 20 kg

● > 80 kg:
for every 10 kg > 80 kg, increase rate by 100 mL/hr

● 100-120 Compressions / minute
● Rhythm / Pulse check every 2 minutes

items.name: Epinephrine 1 mg/mL (1:1000) Endotracheal
information: ● Follow with saline flush or dilute in 1-5 mL isotonic saline

items.name: Adenosine IV/IO
information: ● Subsequent doses 0.2 mg/kg (Max 12 mg), give RAPIDLY
● Follow immediately with 5-10 mL saline flush

items.name: Amiodarone IV/IO
information: ● For perfusing rhythm: give over 20-60 minutes.
● For V-Fib or pulseless V-Tach, give as rapid bolus.

items.name: Atropine IV/IO
information: ● May repeat dose once in 5 minutes.
● Max total dose for child in 1 mg, max total dose for adolescent is 3 mg.

items.name: Atropine Endotracheal
information: ● Max total dose for child is 1 mg, max total dose for adolescent is 3 mg.

items.name: Calcium Gluconate 10% IV/IO
information: ● Substitute for Calcium Chloride if non-emergent situation or no central access.
● In emergencies (cardiac arrest), Calcium Chloride is recommended.

items.name: Cardioversion
information: ● Subsequent settings if first dose ineffective

items.name: Lidocaine Infusion IV/IO
information: 4 mg/mL in D5W or 8 mg/mL in D5W

items.name: Magnesium Sulfate IV/IO
information: ● Give over 10-20 minutes in torsades or V-tach with pulse, asthma, hypomagnesemia
● Give as bolus over 1-2 minutes in pulseless rhythm.

items.name: Procainamide IV/IO
information: ● Give over 30-60 minutes as slow IV injection in solution, monitor QT interval.

items.name: Phenylephrine Bolus IV/IO
information: ● Mix 1 mL (10 mg) of Phenylephrine (10 mg/mL) into 100 mL NS

steps.algostep: ● No pulse, flat line on monitor
● No pulse, non-shockable rhythm on monitor

● 100-120 compressions / minute
● Rhythm / Pulse check every 2 minutes
Slow HR with poor perfusion, altered mental status, hypotension, or shock.
● Oxygen and assist breathing as needed
● Start b/CPR/b if HR < 60 and signs of poor perfusion, rate 100-120 compressions / minute
**** Min 0.1 mg, max 0.5 mg, may repeat x1
● (If wide complex and NO pulse, treat as ventricular tachycardia with defibrillation)
● Begin at 0.5 - 1 J/kg; increase to 2 J/kg if ineffective
● Consider b/Adenosine/b if rhythm and QRS monomorphic
●  First dose 0.1 mg/kg rapid IVP, 2nd dose 0.2 mg/kg rapid IVP if necessary
● First dose 0.1 mg/kg rapid IVP, 2nd dose 0.2 mg/kg rapid IVP if necessary
● Second dose: 0.2 mg/kg rapid IVP (max 12 mg)
● Consider b/synchronized cardioversion/b if no vascular access or child becomes unstable -
● Begin at 0.5-1 J/kg; increase to 2 J/kg if ineffective
● No pulse, fibrillation pattern / no organized electrical complexes
● No pulse, rapid wide QRS complex
● b/CPR/b with rhythm / pulse check every 2 minutes, rate 100-120 compressions / minute
● Continue b/CPR/b for 2 minutes then rhythm / pulse check
● CPR for 2 minutes then rhythm / pulse check

items.name: CUFFED Endotracheal Tube
information: ● Cuffed preferred unless < 1 year of age.

items.name: UNCUFFED Endotracheal Tube
information: ● Cuffed preferred unless < 1 year of age.

information: ● Appropriate endotracheal tube depth should be confirmed by Chest X-Ray as soon as feasible.

information: ** King Airway sizes 0, 1, 2, and 2.5 are not FDA approved for pediatric use in the US**

items.vals: 3,12 french,11,14 french,13,18 french,15,20 french,17,22 french,22,24 french,30,26 french,43,30 french

items.vals: 2.9,5-8 french,8,6-8 french,12,8 french,14,8-10 french,22,10 french,34,10-12 french,50,12-14 french

items.name: Central / Femoral IV
items.vals: 2.9,1.9-3.0 french / 6-8 cm,12,3.0 french / 8 cm,50,5.0 french / 15 cm

items.name: Ibuprofen PO
information: ● Max single dose 400 mg, max daily dose 2400 mg

items.name: Maintenance Infusion Rate
information: 4 mL/kg for 1st 10 kg of weight, + 2 mL/kg for next 10 kg weight, plus 1 mL/kg after that.

items.name: Hypoglycemia
notes: ● Currently defined as: if patient < 24 hours old, serum glucose < 30-40 mg/dL (<1.65-2.2 mol/L)
● if patient is > 24 hours old, serum glucose < 50 mg/dL (< 2.8 mmol/L)

items.name: D10 IV/IO
information: ● D10 is preferred for all pediatric patients

items.name: Glucagon IM/IV/SubQ
information: ● Avoid in infants small for gestational age
May repeat Q15 min x 3 doses, give IV doses over 1-5 minutes
items.brand: Glucagen, Glucagon Emergency Kit, GlucaGen HypoKit, Gvoke

items.name: Dextrose Gel 40% (oral)
information: ● Infants < 12 months: massage into buccal mucosa, repeat if still hypoglycemic after 30 minutes
● Children > 12 months: repeat dose in 10-15 minutes if still hypoglycemic

dosages.label: ● Infants < 6 months, altered mental status, or compromised breathing

items.name: Fentanyl Intranasal
information: ● Avoid in children < 6 months of age or < 10 kg.
● Administer 1/2 dose in each nostril, max 1 mL/nostril

items.name: Hydromorphone IV/IO/IM
information: ● Avoid in infants < 6 months or < 10 kg
● Give IV over 2-3 minutes, can also be given IM (not recommended)

items.name: Hydrocodone / Acetaminophen PO
items.brand: Vicodin, Lorcet, Hycet, Norco, Lortab Elixir, Anexsia, Vicodin ES, Vicodin HP, Xodol, Zamicet

items.name: Oxycodone / Acetaminophen PO
items.brand: Percocet, Primlev, Roxicet, Endocet, Xartemis XR

items.name: Oxycodone PO
items.brand: OxyContin, Xtampza ER, Roxicodone, Oxaydo, RoxyBond

items.name: Lidocaine IO
information: ● For pain related to IO line placement, infuse IO line
● Can repeat Q45 minutes, not to exceed 3 mg/kg/day

items.name: Pressure Management
notes: ● Disclaimer: All pressure support medications should preferably be administered via central venous access

steps.algostep:
● Draw up 0.01 mg/kg epinephrine (0.1 mg/mL 1:10,000), in 10 mL syringe 
●  Mix the below volume with normal saline for a total of 10 mL. 
● Concentration is equivalent to 0.5-2 mcg/kg/dose
● Administer 0.5-2 mL per dose IV/IO every 1-5 minutes

● Mix the above volume with normal saline for a total of 10 mL. 
● Administer 0.5-2 mL per dose IV/IO every 1-5 minutes

● Take a 10 mL syringe with 9 mL of normal saline
● Into this syringe, draw up 1 mL of epinephrine (0.1 mg/mL, 1:10,000)
● Dose is equivalant to 10 mcg/mL.
● Max recommended dose is 20 mcg (2 mL)

items.name: Phenylephrine BOLUS
information: ● Mix 1 mL (10 mg) of Phenylephrine (10 mg/mL) into 100 mL NS

items.name: Dopamine Infusion
information: ● no utility in low dose rate for renal perfusion
**may have higher mortality in pediatric septic shock**

items.name: Milrinone Loading Dose
information: Risk for hypotension, give over 10-60 minutes

items.name: Procedural Sedation
notes: ● Disclaimer: Any pediatric patient undergoing procedural sedation should be closely monitored including telemetry, non-invasive blood pressure, pulse oximetry, and end-tidal capnometry

items.name: Propofol IV BOLUS
information: ● Repeat 0.5 mg/kg Q 3-5 minutes until adequate level of sedation reached.

items.name: "Ketofol" IV
information: ●  Most common recommendation is Ketamine 0.5 mg/kg IV followed by Propofol 0.5 mg/kg IV.
● Additional doses of Propofol 0.5 mg/kg can be given every 2-5 minutes as needed for continued sedation.

items.name: Ketamine IV
information: ● May repeat 0.5-1 mg/kg if inadequate sedation in 5-15 minutes

items.name: Ketamine IM
information: May repeat 2.5-5 mg/kg if inadequate sedation in 5-10 minutes

items.name: Ketamine Intranasal
information: ● Use highest concentration available, recommend 1 mL max per nostril

items.name: Atropine IV/IO
information: consider using in conjunction with Ketamine

items.name: Midazolam IV/IM
information: ● Reduce dose by 30-50% if combined with narcotic

items.name: Midazolam PO
information: ● most common recommended dose is 0.5 mg/kg

items.name: Methohexital IV
information: ● may repeat at doses of 0.5 mg/kg to max dose of 2 mg/kg

items.name: Flumazenil IV
information: for benzodiazepine reversal, use with caution, may precipitate seizures

items.name: Croup
notes: ● Supplemental humidified oxygen to maintain oxygen saturation > 92%, minimize patient stimulation, agitation, needle sticks.
●  If intubation necessary, consider sizing down ET tube.

items.name: Dexamethasone PO
information: ● IV solution can also be given orally

items.name: Epinephrine Nebulized
information: ● 2.5-5 mg Epi (1 mg/mL,1:1,000) mixed with 4-5 mL Normal Saline OR
● Weight-Based: 0.5 mg/kg Epi (1 mg/mL, 1:1000) (Only for use in young infants, max 5 mg)

items.name: Asthma / Mild
notes: ● PRAM score 0-3

items.name: Albuterol MDI
information: Give 4-8 puffs / dose every 20 minutes for 3 doses, then Q1-4 hours PRN.
items.brand: Proventil HFA, Ventolin HFA, Proair HFA

items.name: Albuterol SVN
information: ● can repeat every 20 minutes for 2 doses, then Q1-4 hours PRN
● max single dose 5 mg, minimum 2.5 mg

items.name: Prednisone
information: ● Administer to those who fail initial inhalation therapy

items.name: Prednisolone PO
information: ● Administer to those who fail initial inhalation therapy
items.brand: Pediapred, FloPred, Orapred, Orapred ODT, Millipred, Millipred DP, Prelone Syrup, Veripred 20

items.name: Dexamethasone PO
information: ● IV solution can also be given orally

items.name: Asthma / Moderate
notes: ● PRAM score: 4-7
● Supplemental oxygen to maintain oxygen saturation > 92%

items.name: Albuterol MDI
items.brand: Proventil HFA, Ventolin HFA, Proair HFA

items.name: Albuterol SVN
information: ● Can repeat every 20 minutes for 3 doses, then Q1-4 hr PRN

items.name: Ipratropium Bromide
information: ● adminster with first three dose of nebulized albuterol

items.name: Albuterol / Ipratropium Bromide
information: 1.5 - 3 mL Q 20 min PRN X 3, then Q 1-4 hours PRN

items.name: Prednisolone PO
items.brand: Pediapred, FloPred, Orapred, Orapred ODT, Millipred, Millipred DP, Prelone Syrup, Veripred 20

items.name: Dexamethasone PO
information: ● IV dose can also be given orally

items.name: Asthma / Severe
notes: ● PRAM score 8-12
● Supplemental oxygen to maintain oxygen saturation > 92%

items.name: Albuterol SVN
information: ● Can repeat every 20 minutes for 3 doses, then Q1-4 hr PRN

items.name: Ipratropium Bromide
information: ● Administer with first three doses of nebulized Albuterol.

items.name: Albuterol / Ipratropium Bromide
information: ● 1.5 - 3 ml Q 20 min PRN X 3, then Q 1-4 hours PRN

items.name: Terbutaline SubQ /IM
information: ● Max dose 0.4 mg (0.4 mL of 1 mg/mL solution).
● Can also be given IM, may repeat every 20 minutes x 3 doses
● May give terbutaline OR epinephrine, do NOT give both.

items.name: Terbutaline IV/IO
information: ● Give initial bolus followed by infusion

items.name: Magnesium Sulfate IV
information: ● Consider IV fluid bolus to minimize risk of associated hypotension

items.name: Epinephrine 1 mg/mL (1:1000) IM / SubQ
information: ● Max dose 0.4 mg (0.4 mL of 1 mg/mL solution)

items.name: Midazolam Intranasal
information: ● Use higher concentration (5 mg/mL) when available

items.name: Diazepam Rectal
information: ● Used when IV access is not available
● Max dose 20 mg for each age group

items.name: Levetiracetam IV Loading Dose
information: ● Use 60 mg/kg for status epilepticus, lower dosing approved for other indications.
items.brand: Keppra, Keppra XR, Spritam, Elepsia XR

items.name: Valproic Acid IV Loading Dose
information: ● Adminster at rate of 1.5-3 mg/kg/min, typically provided in diluted solution
● Dose 20-40 mg/kg, use 40 mg/kg for Status epilepticus

items.name: Fosphenytoin IV Loading Dose
information: ● May repeat with additional 5 PE/kg in 10 min

items.name: Phenytoin IV Loading Dose
information: ● May repeat with additional 5-10 mg/kg in 10 minutes, give at rate of 50 mg/min
● Use fosphenytoin in patient in status epilepticus or hemodynamically unstable.

items.name: Phenobarbital IV Loading Dose
information: ● May repeat with additional 5-10 mg/kg in 10 minutes.

items.name: Shock
notes: ● Disclaimer: All pressure support medications should preferably be administered via central venous access

steps.algostep:
● Draw up 0.01 mg/kg epinephrine (0.1 mg/mL 1:10,000), in 10 mL syringe 
● Mix the below volume with normal saline for a total of 10 mL. 
● Concentration is equivalent to 0.5-2 mcg/kg/dose
● Administer 0.5-2 mL per dose IV/IO every 1-5 minutes

● Mix the above volume with normal saline for a total of 10 mL. 
•Administer 0.5-2 mL per dose IV/IO every 1-5 minutes

● Take a 10 mL syringe with 9 mL of normal saline
● Into this syringe, draw up 1 mL of epinephrine (0.1 mg/mL, 1:10,000)
● Dose is equivalent to 10 mcg/mL.
● Max recommended dose is 20 mcg (2 mL)

items.name: Phenylephrine Bolus
information: ● Mix 1 mL (10 mg) of Phenylephrine (10 mg/mL) into 100 mL NS

items.name: Dopamine Infusion
information: ● no utility in low dose rate for renal perfusion
**may have higher mortality in pediatric septic shock**

steps.algostep: ● Theophyine, phenobarbital, digoxin, propoxyphene, nadalol, phenytoin, diazepam, TCAis, chloropropamide, NSADS, salicylates.
● Arsenic, bromide, K+, toxic alcohol, heavy metals

items.name: Cathartics
notes: Use one time with 1st dose charcoal

items.name: Cathartics (Na / MgSo4)
information: ● use one time with first dose charcoal

steps.algostep: ● Dangerous ingestions < 1 hour old, toxins that slow GI transit, decrease mental status or cause seizure, consider with toxins bound poorly by charcoal
● WARNING: Gastric lavage should rarely, if ever, be performed.
● Caustics (acids, alkalis), solvents, hydrocarbons, nontoxic ingestions

items.name: Polyethylene Glycol
information: ● Stop when effluent clear or substance removed

steps.algostep: ● Iron, zinc, lithium, sustained release medications, ingested cocaine, or drug packets
● CNS or respiratory depression, ileus, bowel obstruction, perforation

items.name: Hypoglycemia
notes: ● Currently defined as: if patient is less than 24 hours old, serum glucose < 30-40 mg/dL (<1.65-2.2 mmol/L)
● If patient is > 24 hours old, serum glucose < 50 mg/dL (<2.8mmol/L)

items.name: D10 IV
information: D10 is preferred for all pediatric patients

items.name: Glucagon IM/IV/SubQ
information: ● Avoid in infants smaller for gestational age
● May repeat Q15 min x 3 doses
items.brand: Glucagen, Glucagon Emergency Kit, GlucaGen HypoKit, Gvoke

items.name: Dextrose Gel 40% (oral)
information: ● Infants < 12 months: Massage into buccal mucosa, repeat if still hypoglycemic after 30 minutes.
● Children > 12 months: Repeat dose in 10-15 minutes if still hypoglycemic

items.name: Naloxone Intranasal
information: ● For Intranasal, use highest concentration available
● Prepared intranasal solutions range from 3 - 8 mg per actuation

items.name: Naloxone Continuous Infusion
information: Alternative dosing: Give 2/3rds of initial effective bolus dose as the hourly infusion.

items.name: Acetylcysteine IV
information: ● The loading dose should be infused at 3.75 mL/kg (150 mg/kg) over 15 minutes
** up to 18% of patients develop anaphylactoid reaction**
● Watch for signs of allergic reaction / anaphylaxis.

items.name: Hypotension
information: ● Defined as SBP < 70 + 2 x (age in years)

items.name: Hypertonic Saline (3%) IV/IO
information: ● For use in severe traumatic brain injury or herniation, elevated ICP

items.name: Mannitol IV/IO
information: ● For severe traumatic brain injury, elevated ICP, signs of herniation

● Follow immediately with 5-10 mL saline flush

items.name: Amiodarone
information: ● For perfusing rhythm: give over 20-60 minutes.
● For V-Fib or pulseless V-Tach, give as rapid bolus.

items.name: Atropine IV/IO
information: ● May repeat dose once in 5 minutes.
● Max total dose for child in 1 mg, max total dose for adolescent is 3 mg.

items.name: Calcium Gluconate 10% IV/IO
information: ● Substitute for Calcium Chloride if non-emergent situation or no central access.
● In emergencies (cardiac arrest), Calcium Chloride is recommended.

items.name: Cardioversion
information: ● Subsequent settings if first dose ineffective

items.name: Midazolam Intranasal
information: ● Use higher concentration (5 mg/mL) when available

items.name: Levetiracetam IV/IO (Load)
information: ● Use 60 mg/kg for status epilepticus, lower dosing approved for other indications.
items.brand: Keppra, Keppra XR, Spritam, Elepsia XR

items.name: Dextrose (D10) IV/IO
information: ● D10 is preferred for all pediatric patients

items.name: Magnesium Sulfate IV/IO
information: ● Give over 10-20 minutes in torsades or V-tach with pulse, asthma, hypomagnesemia
● Give as bolus over 1-2 minutes in pulseless rhythm.

items.name: Naloxone Intranasal
information: ● For Intranasal, use highest concentration available
● Prepared intranasal solutions range from 3 - 8 mg per actuation

items.name: EMSC
notes: The Emergency Medical Services for Children (EMSC) Program is a federally funded grant program working to improve emergency care for children across the continuum, from prehospital to emergency department settings. The cornerstone of the EMSC Program is pediatric readiness, which aims to ensure all EMS agencies and emergency departments, no matter their size, pediatric patient volume, or geographic location, have the capability to provide high-quality care for children. Research shows high pediatric readiness in EDs saves lives and is associated with a four-fold lower mortality rate in critically ill children. The EMSC Program offers free resources, toolkits, quality improvement opportunities, and more to improve pediatric readiness, and the capability of all EMS agencies and EDs to provide high-quality care for children.
For more information or access to our free resources and programs, learn more at l/https://www.emscimprovement.center/l.
